channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Cris Villanueva Wins P50,000 in Kapamilya Deal or No Deal Appearance
item.description: Actor Cris Villanueva successfully brought home P50,000 after joining the popular game show “Kapamilya Deal or No Deal.” The episode was featured by ABS-CBN Studios and published on May 23, 2026. The ...

item.title: France temporarily halts additional EU border checks at Dover after severe holiday traffic delays
item.description: French authorities have temporarily suspended additional EU border processing measures at the Port of Dover following significant traffic congestion and lengthy queues affecting thousands of holidayma...

item.title: Research identifies PAHs in common foods and advances methods for detecting cooking-related contaminants
item.description: A recent study from researchers at Seoul National University of Science and Technology highlights the presence of potentially harmful chemical compounds, known as polycyclic aromatic hydrocarbons (PAH...

item.title: Bhopal lawyer Samarth Singh accused in wife Twisha Sharma death case amid investigation
item.description: Samarth Singh, a practising lawyer known for his work in criminal defence and litigation, has come under serious legal scrutiny after being accused in the death case of his wife, Twisha Sharma. Twisha...

item.title: Barbara Parkins: A 1960s Hollywood Actress and Style Icon
item.description: Barbara Parkins, born in 1942 in Vancouver, British Columbia, is a Canadian-American actress who rose to prominence during the 1960s and became widely recognized as both a television and film star as ...

item.title: SpaceX successfully tests upgraded Starship rocket for future Moon and Mars missions
item.description: SpaceX has carried out the 12th test flight of its Starship rocket program, launching the biggest and most powerful version of the spacecraft developed so far. The upgraded Starship V3 lifted off from...

item.title: Health experts warn against frequently delaying urination due to risks to bladder and kidney health
item.description: Medical experts are warning that regularly holding urine for long periods can negatively affect the urinary system, especially the bladder and kidneys. The urinary system is responsible for filtering ...

item.title: Fatal Maldives cave diving incident prompts renewed warnings over training and safety standards
item.description: A cave diving tragedy in the Maldives has led experts to reiterate serious warnings about the dangers of the sport when undertaken without adequate training, planning and specialised equipment. Five d...

item.title: Military forces foil terrorist attack in Kirawa, kill 12 insurgents near Nigeria–Cameroon border
item.description: Troops of the Joint Task Force (North East), under Operation HADIN KAI, have successfully repelled an attempted attack by suspected ISWAP and Boko Haram fighters in the Kirawa axis along the Nigeria–C...

item.title: Why a small kitchen can be practical, efficient and enjoyable
item.description: The article reflects on the author’s experience of living with a compact galley kitchen and how, over time, it has become a space she genuinely appreciates rather than resents. Measuring less than thr...

item.title: Historic 124-year-old church in Regina converted into a bed and breakfast
item.description: A historic church building in Regina has been given a new purpose after being transformed into a bed and breakfast. The 124-year-old structure, which once served as a place of worship for the local co...

item.title: Filipino seafarer arrested in US over alleged 200-kilo cocaine smuggling case
item.description: A Filipino seafarer has been arrested in the United States after being accused of involvement in a large-scale drug trafficking operation involving more than 200 kilograms of cocaine. According to a r...

item.title: Springbok Women dominate Madagascar in Rugby Africa Women's Cup opener with standout debut performances
item.description: The Springbok Women launched their Rugby Africa Women's Cup title defence in emphatic fashion, securing a comprehensive 64–5 victory over Madagascar at the RFUEA Grounds in Nairobi on Saturday afterno...

item.title: MPs Push for Stronger Policies to Protect Local Businesses from Foreign Competition
item.description: Members of Parliament in Tanzania are calling for stronger government action to protect indigenous-owned businesses from increasing competition posed by foreign-owned enterprises operating in the coun...

item.title: Women leaders in Tanzania encouraged to strengthen mentorship and collaboration networks
item.description: Women leaders in Tanzania have been encouraged to strengthen professional solidarity by joining and building platforms that promote experience-sharing, collaboration, and mentorship. The appeal was ma...

item.title: NASA To Open JPL Management Contract To Competition After Decades Under Caltech
item.description: NASA is preparing to open the management contract for the Jet Propulsion Laboratory (JPL) to outside competition for the first time in decades, potentially ending the long-standing relationship betwee...

item.title: Fire at Mitchell industrial building linked to suspected electric vehicle charger fault
item.description: An industrial building in Mitchell, in Canberra’s north, has been damaged after a fire broke out on Saturday morning, with authorities believing an electric vehicle (EV) charger may have been the sour...

item.title: DSE Week 21 2026: Turnover Rises 32% as MKCB Leads Gains; Foreign Investors Remain Net Sellers
item.description: The Dar es Salaam Stock Exchange (DSE) closed Week 21 of 2026 with strong trading activity, recording a significant increase in equity turnover and overall market participation. Total equity turnover ...

item.title: Australia’s political dispute over trust taxation reforms and whether they amount to a ‘death tax’
item.description: A renewed political debate has emerged in Australia over whether proposed changes to trust taxation could be interpreted as a form of ‘death tax’. While Australia formally abolished inheritance taxes ...

item.title: CCTV footage shows suspected abduction of 2-year-old girl in Kahawa Wendani, Nairobi
item.description: A family in Kahawa Wendani, Nairobi County, is in distress after their two-year-old daughter, Linsey Nyakinyua, went missing on Thursday, May 21, in the afternoon. According to reports, the child was ...
